pokemon:vanillite.name: Vanillite
pokemon:vanillite.summary: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.
The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.
Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.
It feels pleasantly cool when embraced. This Pokémon is treasured by households in warm regions.
The temperature of their breath is -58 degrees Fahrenheit. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.
Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.
Born of an icicle, this Pokémon uses its frosty breath to make ice crystals, causing snow to fall.
Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.
When the morning sun hit an icicle, it wished not to melt, and thus Vanillite was born. At night, it buries itself in snow to sleep.
It exhales cold air that is at –58 degrees Fahrenheit. When it’s in a warm place, it shrinks little by little.
This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.
Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.
The temperature of their breath is -58 degrees Fahrenheit. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.
This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

pokemon:vanillish.name: Vanillish
pokemon:vanillish.summary: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.
Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.
They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.
This Pokémon has existed since the Ice Age. It controls particles of ice, freezes its opponents, and then shatters them with a headbutt.
They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.
It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.
Hot days cause its body to melt. It can be restored by refreezing it, but the process leaves its body slightly warped.
By drinking pure water, it grows its icy body. This Pokémon can be hard to find on days with warm, sunny weather.
This hearty Pokémon survived the Ice Age. It’s incredibly popular in very hot regions.
It sprays its enemies with grains of ice to freeze them. Most Vanillish in Alola are smaller than average.
It conceals itself from enemy eyes by creating many small ice particles and hiding among them.
They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.
They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.
Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

pokemon:vanilluxe.name: Vanilluxe
pokemon:vanilluxe.summary: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.
Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.
Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.
Even if it loses one of its heads, it can live relatively problem-free. It makes snow clouds inside its body.
If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow.
People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.
Each of its two heads has a brain, and when they are in agreement, it attacks its enemies by exhaling a violent blizzard.
When its anger reaches a breaking point, this Pokémon unleashes a fierce blizzard that freezes every creature around it, be they friend or foe.
Vanilluxe is born when two Vanillish, half-melted by the day’s light, stick to each other and freeze together in the cold return of night.
It blasts blizzards from its two mouths. It can create snow anywhere, so it gets a lot of love from skiers and snowboarders.
If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow.
Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.
If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow.
Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

loreSummary: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.
